context: Tests whether the agent writes Playwright integration tests using Drama Finder element wrappers correctly: scoped lookups for the 'Notes' dialog field, getTotalRowCount() for grid size assertions, no sleep/waitForTimeout, no getAttribute/isVisible in assertions, proper @Nested/@DisplayName grouping, and correct Drama Finder API usage throughout.

checklist.name: No raw locators
checklist.description: Does NOT use raw Playwright locators such as page.locator("vaadin-text-field") or page.locator("#id") — uses Drama Finder element wrapper factory methods instead

checklist.name: Scoped dialog lookup
checklist.description: The 'Notes' text field inside the Edit Contract dialog is looked up using a scoped factory call — passes dialog.getLocator() (or equivalent container locator) as the scope argument, e.g. TextAreaElement.getByLabel(dialog.getLocator(), "Notes")

checklist.name: getTotalRowCount for grid count
checklist.description: Grid row count assertion uses getTotalRowCount() — does NOT use getRenderedRowCount(), size(), or any row-enumeration approach for the count check

checklist.name: No Thread.sleep / waitForTimeout
checklist.description: Does NOT call Thread.sleep() or page.waitForTimeout() anywhere in the test file

checklist.name: No getAttribute / isVisible in assertions
checklist.description: Does NOT use getAttribute() or isVisible() as the expression being asserted on (i.e. does not appear inside assertThat(), assertEquals(), or assertTrue() calls)

checklist.name: @Nested with @DisplayName
checklist.description: At least one @Nested inner class is present and annotated with @DisplayName

checklist.name: AbstractBasePlaywrightIT base class
checklist.description: The test class extends AbstractBasePlaywrightIT from the Drama Finder library

checklist.name: @SpringBootTest annotation
checklist.description: The test class is annotated with @SpringBootTest(webEnvironment = WebEnvironment.RANDOM_PORT)

checklist.name: getLocator vs getInputLocator distinction
checklist.description: Uses getInputLocator() when checking native input-level attributes (value, maxlength, pattern) and getLocator() for component-level attributes (theme, invalid, opened) — does not mix them up

checklist.name: Icon-only button via getByText
checklist.description: The icon-only close button in the dialog header is located using ButtonElement.getByText(page, "Close") or ButtonElement.getByText(dialog.getLocator(), "Close") — NOT via a CSS selector or raw locator

checklist.name: Drama Finder element wrappers
checklist.description: Uses Drama Finder typed element wrappers (e.g. TextAreaElement, ButtonElement, GridElement, DialogElement, NotificationElement) for all component interactions — at least four distinct element types used
